metadata.analysis_method: CoVe Factored Verification with 5 independent iterations

verification_criteria.evidence_requirement: Explicit textual evidence in source data

metadata.graph_description: Log4j vulnerability attack graph centered on CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell)

data.description: Log4Shell - JNDI injection via log messages enabling remote code execution
data.precondition: Attacker control over log messages/parameters + message lookup substitution enabled
data.postcondition: Remote code execution via LDAP/JNDI lookup

data.description: Incomplete fix for CVE-2021-44228 in version 2.15.0
data.precondition: Control over Thread Context Map (MDC) + non-default Pattern Layout
data.postcondition: Information leak and RCE in some environments

data.description: Uncontrolled recursion from self-referential lookups in Thread Context Map
data.precondition: Control over Thread Context Map data + crafted self-referential lookup string
data.postcondition: Denial of service through uncontrolled recursion

data.description: JMSAppender JNDI injection in Log4j 1.2 - similar to Log4Shell
data.precondition: JMSAppender configured + attacker write access to Log4j configuration
data.postcondition: Remote code execution via JNDI requests

data.description: JMSSink JNDI injection in Log4j 1.x - similar to CVE-2021-4104
data.precondition: JMSSink configured + write access to configuration or attacker-controlled LDAP
data.postcondition: Remote code execution via JNDI requests

data.precondition: JDBC Appender configured with JNDI LDAP data source + attacker control of target LDAP server
TCP/UDP socket server enabled for receiving serialized log events
SocketServer listening to untrusted network traffic + deserialization gadget present
Processing untrusted serialized data in Chainsaw

data.description: Chainsaw deserialization - same issue as CVE-2020-9493
data.affected_version: Log4j 1.2.x with Chainsaw component (prior to Chainsaw V2.0)
data.precondition: Chainsaw component processing untrusted serialized data

links.description: CVE-2021-45046 was an incomplete fix for CVE-2021-44228 in Log4j 2.15.0, allowing continued exploitation in non-default configurations
links.evidence: Explicit statement: 'the fix to address CVE-2021-44228 in Apache Log4j 2.15.0 was incomplete'

links.description: Both vulnerabilities exploit JNDI injection mechanisms for remote code execution
links.evidence: CVE-2021-4104 explicitly states 'remote code execution in a similar fashion to CVE-2021-44228'

links.description: Both use JNDI requests through Log4j 1.x appenders (JMSAppender vs JMSSink)
links.evidence: CVE-2022-23302 explicitly states 'remote code execution in a similar fashion to CVE-2021-4104'

links.description: Identical deserialization issue in Apache Chainsaw component across different Log4j versions
links.evidence: CVE-2022-23307 states 'CVE-2020-9493 identified a deserialization issue... where the same issue exists'

links.description: Both exploit JNDI LDAP mechanisms in Log4j2 for remote code execution
links.evidence: Both descriptions mention JNDI and LDAP exploitation for RCE

links.description: Both exploit Thread Context Map in consecutive Log4j2 patch versions
links.evidence: Both require control over Thread Context Map data, affecting versions 2.15.0 and 2.16.0 respectively

links.description: Both exploit socket server deserialization for remote code execution
links.evidence: Both descriptions detail socket server components deserializing untrusted data leading to RCE
